meta_title: For Founders: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Claude Code Skills Setup to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Claude Code and Antigravity to build a high-impact portfolio from scratch. A beginner-friendly guide for founders to ship and monetize faster.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends by building micro-SaaS projects that solve real pain points. By leveraging Claude Code for rapid architecture and Antigravity for automated boilerplate, you can deploy a professional-grade product in hours. Focus on utility over complexity to demonstrate competence, drive user adoption, and generate immediate revenue.
content.intro:
The silence of an empty portfolio is deafening for a founder. You have the vision, the drive, and the technical hunger, but the market demands proof—a trail of shipped code that validates your ability to execute. Most developers waste months building 'to-do' apps that nobody wants, while successful founders build micro-utilities that solve specific, high-friction problems.

We are shifting the paradigm today. By integrating Claude Code into your workflow, you move from manual keyboard-mashing to high-level architectural orchestration. This isn't just about writing code; it's about building a digital asset that proves your worth while solving a problem people are willing to pay for. Let's build your first piece of social proof.

steps.title: Define the High-Value MVP Scope
steps.description: Don't build a platform; build a utility. Identify a single, painful manual task and scope a tool that automates it. This keeps your portfolio project focused and impressive.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Define a micro-SaaS MVP scope for a tool that solves [Insert Problem]. Focus on 3 core features, user authentication, and Stripe integration for monetization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Draft a project requirements document for a [Project Name] MVP that handles [Core Feature].'

steps.title: Bootstrap Architecture with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a production-ready boilerplate. Stop configuring build tools and start writing the logic that makes your product unique.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a full-stack Next.js 14, Tailwind, and Supabase boilerplate. Include automated tests and a clean folder structure optimized for rapid scaling.

steps.title: Implement Business Logic via Claude
steps.description: Feed your requirements into Claude Code to generate the core functionality. Maintain control by reviewing every module it proposes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement the core logic for [Feature Name]. Ensure it follows clean code principles and includes error handling for edge cases.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create the user service module to handle authentication and database interactions for the subscription model.'

steps.title: Automate Testing and QA
steps.description: A portfolio project is useless if it's broken. Use Claude to write unit tests that prove your code is robust and reliable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write comprehensive unit tests for the newly created services. Focus on edge cases and security vulnerabilities.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Run existing test suite and generate missing tests for the authentication flow.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Ship the product. A live URL is worth more than a thousand lines of local code. Connect your domain and go live.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide the steps to deploy this repository to Vercel with environment variable configuration for Supabase and Stripe.

pro_tips: Always record a 60-second Loom demo of your project; founders hire based on how you explain the solution, not just the code.
Include a 'Build Log' in your README.md to show your thought process—it’s the best way to demonstrate senior-level architectural thinking.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that solves a specific financial pain point; investors and clients ignore generic crud apps.
Use Claude Code to refactor your code for performance after the MVP is functional; show that you care about technical debt.
Always add a 'Roadmap' section to your project site to show you think in terms of long-term product evolution, not just one-off tasks.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, share it on X and LinkedIn with a focus on the problem you solved. Reach out to 5 potential users for feedback, iterate based on their input, and watch your portfolio grow from a single project into a collection of validated assets.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop waiting for permission and start building your reputation as a high-velocity founder. Don't just watch the AI revolution—lead it. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
